task:
Close the integration-seam gap for the machine-local cache retention engine by building a real-wiring proof harness that exercises the daemon's actual boot entry `runRetentionGcOnce(ctx)` (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:2664, called at boot from packages/daemon/src/server.ts:237) against a SCRATCH COPY of a real store and real run-dirs with retention enabled ONLY in that scratch.

WHY: The existing scripts/proof-retention.mjs proves the engine in isolation but RE-BUILDS the deps by hand; it never calls runRetentionGcOnce, so the real boot path (readSettings -> list runs -> resolve workspace paths via findWorkspace -> bind real store + real run-dirs together -> runRetentionGc -> appendAudit) has never actually run with the flag ON. That wiring is the documented soft spot.

WHAT TO BUILD: a new Node ESM harness at scripts/proof-retention-integration.mjs that:
1. Creates a FRESH temp working dir via fs.mkdtemp (e.g. under os.tmpdir()). This is the scratch cocoderHome.
2. Copies a real store + run-dir set INTO that scratch: copy the install's local/cocoder.db (and -wal/-shm if present) and a representative set of local/runs/<workspaceId>/<runId> scratch dirs, plus the workspace registry / settings needed for findWorkspace to resolve, from the install's local/ directory. SOURCE IS READ-ONLY: copy OUT of the real install; NEVER write back to or mutate the real install's local/. All mutation happens only inside the temp scratch.
3. Writes settings.json into the scratch with retention.enabled: true (default N=25) -- enabled ONLY in the scratch, never on the live install.
4. Constructs a REAL OzContext pointed at the scratch cocoderHome (real store opened on the scratch db, runsRoot = scratch local/runs) and calls the REAL exported runRetentionGcOnce(ctx) -- do NOT re-implement or hand-build the RetentionGcDeps the way proof-retention.mjs does; the whole point is to drive the real seam including readSettings, findWorkspace resolution, projectionCheckerFor, and appendAudit.
5. Runs the pass TWICE to prove idempotency (second pass prunes nothing new and does not grow state).
6. Measures and prints BEFORE/AFTER: cocoder.db size, -wal size, total run-dir count, and retained-run count per workspace; asserts retained <= N per workspace and total bounded ~= N x workspaces.
7. Asserts the hard safety invariants on the real seam: no non-terminal/protected run pruned; every pruned run's durable record exists in its repo's cocoder/runs/ (projection gate) -- i.e. an unprojected terminal run is KEPT; cross-run fault recurrence survives (listFaultHistory still returns the pre-GC faults after the pass); WAL and audit/turn logs shrink/rotate.
8. Exits 0 with a clear PASS summary and non-zero with a clear FAIL on any violated assertion.

ACCEPTANCE: `node scripts/proof-retention-integration.mjs` runs end-to-end against the real runRetentionGcOnce, exits 0, and prints the before/after footprint, the idempotency result, and explicit PASS lines for each safety invariant (projection gate, protected/non-terminal exclusion, recurrence survival, WAL/log rotation). If the real wiring reveals a defect (e.g. findWorkspace fails to resolve copied workspaces, deps mis-bound, db opened read-only, etc.), STOP and report the exact defect with evidence rather than papering over it -- a real gap found here is a valid outcome, not a failure to hide.

MUST NOT BREAK / CONSTRAINTS: do NOT modify the real install's local/ (read-only source copy only); do NOT enable retention on the live install; do NOT change retention engine behavior in this atom (harness only -- packages/core/src/runner/retention*.ts and the launcher seam stay as-is unless you find a genuine wiring defect, in which case report it for a separate atom); do NOT restart the daemon or run git. Build/typecheck must stay green. Report the exact commands you ran and their real exit codes and the printed footprint numbers as evidence.
